personal_info.name: Hyunmin Kim, Ph.D.
personal_info.department: Department of Genetics and Genome Sciences

professional_summary: Computational biologist with 20+ years of experience in regulomics, RNA processing, and 3D genomics.
Author of 30+ publications, including first-author papers in Nature Structural & Molecular Biology, Genes & Development, and Molecular Cell.
Specialized in the integrative analysis of multi-omic data (RNA-seq, ChIP-seq, HiChIP, CNV) to dissect gene-regulatory mechanisms and drug effects at single-cell and spatial resolution.
Developer of multiple open-source bioinformatics pipelines for 3D genome modeling, alternative splicing, and epigenomic analysis.
Proven experience in mentorship, cross-disciplinary collaboration, and HPC/cloud-based pipeline development (GCP, Terra, Nextflow, Docker).

education.institution: University of Colorado School of Medicine
University of Colorado Denver School of Medicine

professional_experience.role: Senior Research Associate – Specialized Technical Skills
responsibilities: Lead development of CNV-integrated and 3D chromatin–aware ChIP-seq/HiChIP pipelines for enhancer–promoter architecture analysis.
Conduct integrative multi-omics (WGS, ATAC-seq, RNA-seq, HiChIP) across neural differentiation and cancer models.
Mentor trainees and contribute to NIH-funded studies on transcriptomic misregulation and clonal hematopoiesis.
Directed development of AI-driven biomedical data-mining tools for translational genomics.
Built Google Cloud–based pipelines and scalable data-storage systems for multi-omics and single-cell datasets.
Led computational analysis team integrating single-cell, spatial transcriptomics, and bulk RNA-seq for immuno-oncology.

professional_experience.organization: University of Colorado Cancer Center & School of Medicine
responsibilities: Developed computational tools for alternative splicing and polyadenylation detection.
Co-led multi-lab studies on transcription termination and enhancer regulation.
Served as Bioinformatics Lead and Co-Investigator on NIH/NCI and V Foundation grants.

selected_projects.name: Exosome-scRNA-organoid
selected_projects.description: Exosome mutation effects on cerebellum development, parse-seq single-cell
